entries.definition:
ق/م/ط/ر q–m–ṭ–r a sturdy, strong, fast-moving camel, short strong
man; to reach a crisis; to scowl, to look angry; to pull tight the
mouth of a water skin. Of this root, قَمْطَرِير qamṭarīr occurs once
in the Qur’an.

قَمْطَرِير qamṭarīr [quasi-act. part.] grim, difficult, stressful,
calamitous (76:10) إِنَّا نَخَافُ مِنْ رَبِّنَا يَوْمًا عَبُوسًا قَمْطَرِيرًا indeed, we fear
from our Lord a scowling, grim Day.
